CrossHire · Databricks system-tables audit

The query library, explained

95 copy-paste SQL queries that audit a Databricks account's cost, performance, compute, jobs, serving, storage and governance — built entirely on system.* tables. No app to install, no dashboard to wire up: open a query, read what it does and how to read the result, copy the SQL, and run it.

95 queries7 domains★ 10 first-audit picks

Who this is for

Pick your seat — each role has a cluster of domains that pays off first.

FinOps & cost owners

Find where the bill actually goes, prove the negotiated discount is landing, charge back by team, and hunt recoverable waste.

Cost Jobs Compute Storage

Platform & workspace admins

Right-size compute, move jobs off expensive all-purpose clusters, and shut down idle warehouses and dormant serving endpoints.

Compute Jobs Serving

Data engineers

Tune the heaviest queries with real pruning / spill / shuffle signals, harden pipeline reliability, and keep table maintenance efficient.

Performance Jobs Storage

Security, governance & compliance

Audit grants, masks and row filters, trace how far PII propagates, and catch run-as escalation and network exfiltration attempts.

Governance

The library, by domain

Seven domains. Click one to see its queries, or jump straight to a query from the sidebar.

DBU spend dollarized by SKU, product, job, notebook, endpoint, tag and identity; negotiated-vs-list price; egress and GenAI tokens. Failures and wasted DBUs, all-purpose placement, missing timeouts, queueing, cold starts, retries and orphans. Model Serving & AI4What's running on Model Serving, how much traffic and token throughput each endpoint handles, which endpoints are dormant (paying but idle), and whether AI Gateway is seeing abuse. Clustering / compaction / VACUUM history, table inventory, time-travel retention and Iceberg / UniForm metadata. Grants, column masks and row filters, tags, data classification, lineage blast-radius, network denials and run-as escalation.

★ The first audit

New to the account? These ten are the highest-signal queries to run first — the biggest money and the biggest risk.

Negotiated vs List Discount Realizationcost_actual_vs_list_by_sku Per-Job DBU Cost Rankingcost_by_job Chargeback Coverage by Tagcost_chargeback_by_tag Dollarizing DBUs at List Pricecost_dollarized_by_sku_day Serverless, Photon & Tier Premiumscost_premium_serverless_photon Your Heaviest Statements, Rankedquery_costly_statements Warehouse Idle-Tail Secondscompute_warehouse_idle_gaps Failing Jobs Ranked by Wasted DBUslakeflow_failed_jobs_wasted_dbus Job Tasks On All-Purpose Computelakeflow_jobs_on_all_purpose Run-As Privilege Escalation Watchaccess_runas_escalation

How to run

  1. Open a Databricks SQL editor (or a notebook on a SQL warehouse / UC-enabled cluster).
  2. Make sure you have SELECT on the system.* schema the query reads — some schemas must be enabled by an account admin first.
  3. Open a query here, copy its SQL, set the look-back window on the :period_days parameter, and run.
Best-effort by design. Databricks system tables aren't uniformly available: a query may return nothing or raise TABLE_OR_VIEW_NOT_FOUND / insufficient_privileges on your account — that's expected, not a bug. Every dollar figure is est · at list (pre-discount, DBU-only) unless a query joins account_prices, and usage_quantity is DBUs / bytes / hours / tokens — never dollars. Sample-output tables are illustrative synthetic rows, never real account data.
Overview / Cost & Billing

Cost & Billing

23 queries

Where the money goes — and whether you can trust the number. DBU spend dollarized by SKU, product, job, notebook, endpoint, tag and identity; negotiated-vs-list price; egress and GenAI tokens.

System tables reference & sources: queries/cost/README.md ↗

01Negotiated vs List Discount Realization ★cost_actual_vs_list_by_skuPer-SKU net DBUs alongside their negotiated-rate dollars and list-rate dollars, so an admin can quantify how much the account's negotiated discount actually saves on each SKU. 02Per-Job DBU Cost Ranking ★cost_by_jobNet DBU consumption and distinct run count per job per day, split by workspace, cloud, product line, and classic-vs-serverless placement. 03Chargeback Coverage by Tag ★cost_chargeback_by_tagNet DBU consumption and record count broken down by every custom_tags key/value pair per usage_date, cloud, workspace, and product line — with untagged usage retained as NULL-key rows so the "% untagged" denominator is real. 04Dollarizing DBUs at List Price ★cost_dollarized_by_sku_dayNet DBU (and other-unit) consumption multiplied by the point-in-time list rate to produce a pre-discount list-dollar figure (net_list_cost) per usage_date x cloud x SKU x product x usage_type x unit x currency. 05Serverless, Photon & Tier Premiums ★cost_premium_serverless_photonNet DBU consumption per usage_date x cloud x SKU x product line, split out by the serverless, Photon, jobs/SQL/DLT tier, and serverless performance-target choices so each premium lever can be sized. 06Your Real Negotiated Price Basis cost_account_prices_rawThe full history of your account's actual negotiated per-SKU rates, one row per price change, kept raw so downstream logic can price each usage record in its own validity window. 07Where the Money Goes by Product cost_by_billing_origin_productNet metered consumption over the trailing window, broken out by Databricks product line, unit of measure, and cloud, with a record count per group. 08Cost Per Cluster and Warehouse cost_by_compute_resourceA daily, per-workspace breakdown of net DBU consumption attributed to each individual cluster, SQL warehouse, and instance pool, split by product line and serverless-vs-classic. 09Ad-hoc notebook DBU cost cost_by_notebookNet DBU consumption per notebook-attached interactive/all-purpose notebook, sliced by day, cloud, workspace, product line, and serverless-vs-classic. 10Serving & Vector Spend by Endpoint cost_by_serving_endpointA daily breakdown of Model Serving and Vector Search DBU consumption per endpoint, workspace, and usage type, so you can see which AI endpoints drive the bill. 11Chargeback DBUs by Identity cost_chargeback_by_identityNet DBU consumption rolled up per identity (human user vs service principal, with masked run-as / owner / creator) sliced by day, cloud, workspace, and product line. 12The Non-DBU Cloud Spend cost_cloud_infraA daily rollup of the cloud provider's own infrastructure and network/egress charges (real billed dollars, not DBUs) broken out by cloud and currency over the trailing window. 13DBSQL Allocation Gap Check cost_dbsql_allocation_gapTwo labelled daily DBU rollups for SQL-warehouse (DBSQL) usage in one result set — one from raw billing, one from Databricks' fair-split attribution — so you can difference them to size unattributed / cross-subsidized shared-pool DBUs. 14Default-Storage DSU by API Tier cost_default_storage_dsuNet default-storage (DSU) usage broken out per day, cloud, SKU, storage API tier (TIER_1 vs TIER_2), and catalog. 15GenAI Token & GPU Spend cost_genai_token_gpuNet TOKEN / GPU_TIME / ANSWER consumption per day, broken out by SKU, product line, usage type, serving type, and serving endpoint. 16Billed Data Egress Signal cost_networking_egressOne row per usage-date, cloud, networking SKU, region pair, client, and Delta Sharing recipient, giving the net billed networking volume (bytes or hours) over the trailing window. 17Can You Trust the Bill? cost_restatement_trust_metricPer cloud, the net billed usage alongside its original, retracted, and restated components plus the latest ingestion date, quantifying how much consumption was later corrected and proving the net figure never double-counts those corrections. 18Model-Serving Cost Mode Per Endpoint cost_serving_mode_by_endpointPer-endpoint model-serving consumption over the trailing window, broken out by billed signal (serving type, usage type, and scale-from-zero LAUNCH flag) so each endpoint's cost mode can be inferred, with a bounded, unverified list-dollar figure. 19The Base Billing Rollup cost_totals_by_sku_dayNet metered consumption (DBUs/bytes/tokens/hours) per day, cloud, workspace, SKU, product line, usage type and serverless flag, with the correction-record breakdown that proves the net figure is trustworthy. 20Serverless Policy Coverage Gaps cost_usage_policy_coverageNet DBU consumption bucketed by whether a usage policy is attached (usage_policy / legacy budget_policy / none) and whether custom tags are present, with is_serverless exposed as a dimension (not a filter) so coverage % can be computed over serverless spend, sliced per day, cloud, workspace, and product line. 21Vector Search Spend by Endpoint cost_vector_search_spendDaily Vector Search consumption per endpoint, split into serving/ingest DBUs versus DSU storage, with an unverified list-dollar estimate alongside each raw usage figure. 22Workspace ID to Name Lookup cost_workspace_namesA tiny reference table that resolves each numeric workspace ID to its human name and active/deleted status, one row per workspace. 23The List-Price Rate Book pricing_list_prices_rawThe full history of Databricks public list (pre-discount) prices per SKU, one row per price change, collected raw so the engine can price each usage row in its correct time window.
Overview / Query Performance

Query Performance

11 queries

Which SQL statements are slow, wasteful or failing — and why. Heaviest statements, file pruning, disk spill, shuffle, cache cold-start, queuing, failures and workload mix.

System tables reference & sources: queries/performance/README.md ↗

01Your Heaviest Statements, Ranked ★query_costly_statementsThe top 1000 finished, non-cached SQL statements in the trailing window, ranked by execution time (a DBU-cost proxy), each carrying pruning, spill, shuffle, and scan counters plus a de-valued query text to diagnose the fix. 02What This Audit Costs You audit_self_costA per-workspace, per-statement-type tally of the audit's own queries — how many ran, how long they took in wall-clock and task time, how many principals ran them, and the first/last time they landed — so you can see the cost footprint of running CrossHire itself. 03Cache Hits vs. Cold-Start Latency query_cache_coldstartA daily per-warehouse rollup that separates the two distinct cache signals (result-cache hits and disk/IO cache coverage) from cold-start and compile-bound latency. 04Daily Failed & Canceled Queries query_failed_queries_dailyA daily rollup of FAILED and CANCELED statement executions — counts, wasted runtime, and a de-valued error-message sample — sliced by workspace, warehouse, compute type, statement type, and (masked) user. 05Disk Spill Memory-Pressure Hotspots query_local_spillageA daily rollup of statements that spilled to local disk, showing per day, workspace, compute type, warehouse, and (masked) user how many queries spilled, how much they spilled, and their duration and shuffle context. 06Per-Query Cost Allocation Inputs query_per_query_estimate_laneOne row per finished, non-cached warehouse statement, carrying the raw duration, task-time and scan-bytes drivers (bucketed by hour) that a downstream job weights to estimate each query's share of metered warehouse DBUs. 07Where Query Spend Comes From query_provenance_by_sourceA grouped rollup of the last 30 complete days of statement executions, attributing each to its originating source (job, dashboard, legacy_dashboard, notebook, alert, genie, sql_editor) and identity type (human user vs. service principal), with query counts, execution/wall-clock time, and scan bytes per workspace and warehouse. 08Are Your Scans Skipping Files? query_pruning_effectivenessA daily rollup of files pruned versus files read (plus partitions, bytes, and rows scanned) for scan statements, sliced by workspace, warehouse, user, and statement type, so you can spot workloads doing full scans instead of pruning. 09Capacity Queuing vs Cold-Start Waits query_queuing_waitsA daily, per-warehouse rollup that separates time queries spent queued because a warehouse was at capacity from time they spent waiting for compute to spin up. 10Shuffle And Small-Files Hotspots query_shuffle_write_amplificationA daily rollup of shuffle-read volume and write output (bytes, rows, files) for shuffle-heavy or write-heavy statements, sliced by workspace, compute type, warehouse, masked user, and statement type. 11When And What Runs query_workload_mix_hoursA day-by-hour histogram of query volume, runtime, scan bytes, and rows returned, sliced by workspace, compute type, warehouse, statement type, and (masked) user.
Overview / Compute

Compute

10 queries

What compute exists, how it's configured, and how hard it's actually working. Cluster and SQL-warehouse config, node utilization, idle ratio, autoscale churn, idle tails and pools.

System tables reference & sources: queries/compute/README.md ↗

01Warehouse Idle-Tail Seconds ★compute_warehouse_idle_gapsPer SQL warehouse, the total seconds spent RUNNING (the auto-stop idle tail), STARTING (cold-start tax) and STOPPED, the worst single RUNNING gap, and event counts by state — all derived from state-transition events over the trailing :period_days window. 02Live Classic Cluster Config Baseline classic_clusters_config_currentOne row per live classic compute cluster showing its latest configuration — node types, autoscale range, idle auto-stop, runtime, access mode, policy coverage, pool linkage, tags, init scripts and cloud attributes. 03Idle Classic Clusters, Ranked compute_idle_node_ratioOne row per classic cluster over the lookback window, reporting total vs idle minute-slices (idle = CPU under 5%) alongside average and peak CPU and memory and the observed time span - the ranked auto-stop / auto-termination candidate list. 04SQL Warehouses Thrashing Clusters compute_warehouse_autoscale_churnOne row per SQL warehouse over the trailing window, counting its scale-up and scale-down events, the observed span those events cover, and the max/average clusters running — the raw signal for detecting autoscale churn. 05Spot Mix and Instance Idle Signal instance_events_idle_activeA per-workspace, per-node-type, per-availability/state/event-type roll-up of cloud-VM lifecycle events for classic compute, showing how many events and distinct instances occurred and the first/last time each combination was seen over the trailing window. 06Idle Instance-Pool Waste Snapshot instance_pools_idle_capacityOne row per live instance pool showing its always-on idle floor, capacity ceiling, idle auto-termination, preloaded images and node type — the pre-warmed-VM waste and right-sizing baseline. 07Right-Sizing Evidence From Node Telemetry node_timeline_utilizationOne row per cluster / node-type / driver-vs-worker role summarizing observed CPU, memory, CPU-wait and network from per-minute classic-compute hardware telemetry over a trailing window (≤90 days). 08Node Type Hardware Spec Catalog node_types_referenceA static reference list of every node/instance type available in the account with its vCPU, memory, and GPU specs — the hardware denominator for right-sizing math. 09Live SQL Warehouse Config Baseline sql_warehouse_config_currentOne row per live (non-deleted) SQL warehouse showing its latest configuration — type, channel, size, autoscale range, idle auto-stop, and chargeback tags. 10SQL Warehouse Event Activity sql_warehouse_events_activityOne row per SQL warehouse and event type over the window, giving the event count, the first and last time that event fired, and the peak and average number of clusters running when it did.
Overview / Jobs & Pipelines

Jobs & Pipelines

21 queries

The reliability, ownership, timeout hygiene and cost of Jobs and Lakeflow pipelines. Failures and wasted DBUs, all-purpose placement, missing timeouts, queueing, cold starts, retries and orphans.

System tables reference & sources: queries/jobs_pipelines/README.md ↗

01Failing Jobs Ranked by Wasted DBUs ★lakeflow_failed_jobs_wasted_dbusOne row per job that had at least one failed run in the trailing window, with its distinct/failed run counts, the latest failure's termination code, total job DBUs, and a wasted-DBU proxy (job DBUs scaled by failed-run share). 02Job Tasks On All-Purpose Compute ★lakeflow_jobs_on_all_purposePer-job, per-cluster task-run counts over the trailing 30 days, tagged with the cluster's source so you can spot job tasks running on all-purpose (UI/API) compute instead of cheaper job clusters. 03Job Failure Breakdown by Root Cause lakeflow_failed_runsA 30-day census of completed job runs bucketed by workspace, launch type, trigger, final outcome, and root-cause code, with raw-row, distinct-run, and failed-row counts for each bucket. 04Job Health-Rule Coverage lakeflow_health_rule_coveragePer-workspace counts of active jobs that have a configured health rule versus none, with a separate bucket for jobs where the health_rules column has not been populated yet. 05Orphaned And Handed-Off Jobs lakeflow_job_ownership_orphansPer-workspace counts of active jobs whose creator or run-as identity is missing, whose creator and run-as differ (a handoff or service-principal pattern), or that look orphaned because no known identity runs them. 06Jobs Stuck Waiting On Capacity lakeflow_job_queue_timePer-job queue-wait time (total seconds and approximate p95 per run) over the trailing window, with a separate counter of runs whose queue time is unknown, so admins can find jobs that sit waiting on capacity before they start. 07Task-Level Timeout Coverage Gaps lakeflow_job_tasks_no_timeoutPer-workspace counts of active job tasks and how many of them have no (or zero) task-level timeout configured, with a separate bucket for the "not yet populated" NULL case. 08Jobs Missing a Timeout lakeflow_jobs_no_timeoutPer-workspace counts of active jobs that have no (or zero) job-level timeout configured, alongside a separate "not yet populated" NULL bucket so the finding never over-claims. 09Runs Killed Before They Launched lakeflow_never_started_runsA per-job count of Databricks job runs that were terminated before they ever began executing, broken out by the termination reason, over the trailing 30 days. 10Where Job Time Actually Goes lakeflow_phase_cold_startPer-job phase totals (setup, queue, execution, cleanup, and overall run) plus 95th-percentile setup and queue times over the trailing 30 days, so you can see how much wall-clock time is startup and wait overhead versus real work. 11Per-Pipeline Cost and Over-Refresh lakeflow_pipeline_costOne row per Lakeflow (DLT) pipeline over the trailing window, showing its net DBUs billed, its maintenance DBUs broken out separately, a list-price dollar estimate, and how many times it refreshed and for how long. 12Pipeline Active-Time and Idle-Tail Signal lakeflow_pipeline_idle_tail_durationPer-Lakeflow-pipeline count of updates and total active run seconds over the last 30 days, tagged with each pipeline's type and its continuous/development settings so you can spot pipelines whose clusters likely linger idle between refreshes. 13Pipeline Refresh Failures And Retries lakeflow_pipeline_update_failures_retriesA 30-day breakdown of Lakeflow/DLT pipeline update outcomes, counting how many refreshes ran, how many failed, and how many were fired by retry-on-failure, sliced per workspace, pipeline, update type, trigger, and result state. 14DLT Pipeline Fleet Inventory lakeflow_pipelines_inventory_tierA per-workspace census of your live Lakeflow Declarative (DLT) pipelines, counting how many run in each combination of pipeline type, serverless, development-mode, continuous, and product edition. 15Flapping Jobs That Retry lakeflow_retries_repairsPer-job retry tallies over the trailing 30 days — how many runs each job had, how many extra attempts those runs racked up, and how many runs needed at least one retry. 16Hunting Stale Zombie Jobs lakeflow_stale_zombie_jobsEvery active (non-deleted) job definition with the timestamp its last run was last seen running and a flag marking those that haven't run in over 30 days (or ever). 17Green Runs Hiding Failed Tasks lakeflow_succeeded_with_failed_tasksPer job, how many runs reported overall SUCCEEDED yet actually contained at least one task that FAILED, ERROR'd, or TIMED_OUT over the trailing 30 days. 18Tasks Running Near Timeout lakeflow_tasks_near_timeoutPer-task run statistics over the lookback window flagging task runs that reached at least 80% of (or exceeded) their configured timeout, so you can tune the bound or fix the slowdown before it trips. 19Job Failure Root-Cause Breakdown lakeflow_termination_taxonomyA 30-day tally, per workspace, of how many job runs ended with each termination code — the root-cause taxonomy of why runs stopped. 20Discover Job Termination-Type Values lakeflow_termination_type_probeA runtime discovery probe that lists the distinct raw `termination_type` values appearing on finished job runs per workspace over the last 30 days, with a count of how many end-of-run rows carry each value. 21Daily Job Run Mix And Hours lakeflow_workload_mix_hoursA daily breakdown, per workspace, of how many distinct job runs were active and how many execution-seconds they consumed, sliced by run type and trigger type over the trailing 30 days.
Overview / Model Serving & AI

Model Serving & AI

4 queries

What's running on Model Serving, how much traffic and token throughput each endpoint handles, which endpoints are dormant (paying but idle), and whether AI Gateway is seeing abuse.

System tables reference & sources: queries/serving_ai/README.md ↗

01AI Gateway Abuse Signals compute_ai_gateway_usageA daily per-workspace, per-endpoint, per-requester breakdown of AI Gateway request volume, its success / rate-limited / error split, input/output token throughput, and p50/p95/max latency. 02Idle Serving Endpoints You Pay For compute_serving_dormant_endpointsEvery served entity in its latest configuration, paired with its request traffic over the trailing window, so entities that received no requests surface as dormant (`total_requests = 0`, `last_request_date` NULL). 03Daily Serving Endpoint Traffic compute_serving_endpoint_usageA daily rollup of Model Serving traffic per endpoint and served entity, showing total, successful and errored requests plus input/output token totals, with endpoint and entity names joined from the serving configuration. 04Serving Endpoint Traffic Rollup serving_endpoint_traffic_by_endpointOne row per Model Serving endpoint over the trailing window, giving total requests, the success/error split, input and output token totals, and the date each endpoint was last hit.
Overview / Storage & Optimization

Storage & Optimization

9 queries

What Predictive Optimization is doing to your Delta tables and what it costs. Clustering / compaction / VACUUM history, table inventory, time-travel retention and Iceberg / UniForm metadata.

System tables reference & sources: queries/storage/README.md ↗

01Detecting UniForm & Managed-Iceberg Tables iceberg_uniform_metadataFor a single target table, returns its full extended metadata rows so the "Delta Uniform Iceberg" section can be read to detect whether the table is UniForm / managed-Iceberg enabled. 02Liquid Clustering Work and Cost po_clustering_activityPer managed Unity Catalog table, a 30-day summary of how many liquid/automatic CLUSTERING operations ran, the files and bytes they compacted (removed vs. clustered), and the estimated DBUs that clustering consumed. 03Clustering Key Churn Detector po_clustering_column_churnPer managed table (and per distinct old-to-new/reason signature), the auto-clustering column-selection decisions over the last 30 days — whether the keys changed, the old to new column sets, the stated reason, when it last happened, and how many times. 04Data-Skipping Stats Backfill Tracker po_data_skipping_backfillOne row per managed table and distinct data-skipping column-change set, showing which stat columns Predictive Optimization added, removed, or newly indexed for pruning, plus the bytes and files it scanned to do so, over the trailing 30 days. 05Predictive Optimization Cost & Coverage po_maintenance_cost_by_tableOne row per table x maintenance-type x outcome over the last 30 days, giving the operation count, summed estimated maintenance DBUs, and first/last op times. 06VACUUM Storage Reclaimed Per Table po_vacuum_reclaimed_bytesOne row per managed table showing how many successful VACUUM operations ran in the last 30 days and how many files, bytes, and estimated DBUs they reclaimed. 07Real Table Size Breakdown storage_breakdown_analyzeA single row of one Delta table's true on-disk footprint, split into active data, time-travel history, and reclaimable (vacuumable) bytes and file counts. 08Table Inventory by Type and Format table_inventory_typeA catalog/schema-level census of every table and view the collector can see, counted by managed/external/view type and by data source format. 09Delta Time-Travel & Optimize Settings table_props_time_travel_configFor one managed Delta table, the seven Delta properties that govern time-travel retention, auto-optimize, data-skipping indexing, and deletion vectors, each with its effective value or default.
Overview / Governance, Access & Security

Governance, Access & Security

17 queries

Who can touch what, who did, and where sensitive data leaks its guardrails. Grants, column masks and row filters, tags, data classification, lineage blast-radius, network denials and run-as escalation.

System tables reference & sources: queries/governance_access/README.md ↗

01Run-As Privilege Escalation Watch ★access_runas_escalationA 30-day rollup of audited actions where the initiating principal (run_by) differs from the identity the action executed as (run_as), aggregated per service, action, and masked identity pair with event counts and first/last timestamps. 02Who Changed Permissions, From Where access_admin_role_change_eventsA 90-day rollup of account, access-control, and Unity Catalog audit events grouped by service, action, and acting principal, showing how often each fired, from how many distinct source IPs, and the first/last time it was seen. 03Sensitive Columns Missing Their Mask access_classified_unmaskedA current-state list of HIGH-confidence auto-classified (PII/sensitive) columns, each showing whether a column mask is applied and flagging the ones that are left unmasked. 04Column-Level Lineage Blast Radius access_column_lineage_sensitive_reachA 90-day rollup of observed column-to-column data flows, one row per source-column to target-column edge (also split by entity_type and direct_access), showing how many times each edge fired, how many distinct principals drove it, and when it last happened. 05Which Columns Are Actually Masked access_column_masks_inventoryA current-state inventory listing every column in the metastore that has a column mask applied, naming the mask function and the columns it reads. 06Where Sensitive Data Actually Lives access_data_classification_inventoryA deduplicated inventory of every column the auto-classifier flagged as sensitive, one row per catalog/schema/table/column/class-tag/confidence/data-type, carrying the peak match frequency and first/last detection times. 07Dead-Table Cleanup Candidates access_dead_table_candidatesEvery MANAGED or EXTERNAL table in your account that never showed up as a read (lineage source) during the look-back window, listed with its owner and staleness age as cleanup candidates to investigate. 08Who Holds Which Grants access_grants_inventoryA current-state, privilege-scoped inventory of table- and catalog-level grants, rolled up to one row per object scope, privilege type, and grantee with the number of grants and distinct objects each covers. 09Grants on Schemas and Integrations access_grants_inventory_extendedA privilege map counting who holds which grants on schemas, UC connections, credentials, and external locations, one row per object scope, privilege, and grantee. 10Login Concentration & Anomaly Rollup access_login_concentrationA 30-day rollup of account authentication activity, one row per masked principal x source IP x service x action, with success and non-success event counts and a first/last-seen window. 11Blocked Inbound Network Attempts access_network_inbound_denialsA 30-day rollup of inbound (ingress) network-policy enforcement events, grouped by policy outcome, rule, request path, principal, and source IP, with a count and first/last timestamps per group — the query groups by outcome rather than filtering it, so it surfaces whatever outcomes the table records (DENY / DENY_DRY_RUN denials in practice). 12Blocked Egress Exfiltration Attempts access_network_outbound_denialsA 30-day rollup of outbound (egress) network-policy denials, grouped by source and destination type, access type, and destination, with DNS/storage rejection detail and denial counts. 13PII Leaking Into Untagged Columns access_pii_propagation_untaggedOne row per direct column-lineage flow where a sensitivity-tagged source column fed an untagged target column, naming the responsible principal and how many times it happened. 14Row Filter Coverage Inventory access_row_filters_inventoryA current-state list of every table in the metastore that has a row-level access filter attached, naming the filter and the columns it evaluates. 15Table Lineage Blast Radius access_table_lineage_blast_radiusA 90-day rollup of table-level data-flow edges, each classified as READ, WRITE, READ_WRITE, or UNKNOWN, with how many times it occurred, how many distinct principals drove it, and when it last happened. 16Manual Governance Tag Inventory access_tags_inventoryA rollup of every manual governance tag applied at column and table scope, showing each tag name/value pair and how many objects carry it. 17Vector Search Query Traffic by Endpoint access_vector_search_trafficA daily count of Vector Search query and scan events per endpoint over the trailing window, used to tell which endpoints are actually being used versus provisioned-but-idle.
Overview / Cost & Billing / cost_actual_vs_list_by_sku
01
Cost & Billing cost_actual_vs_list_by_sku ★ first-audit pick

Negotiated vs List Discount Realization

Per-SKU net DBUs alongside their negotiated-rate dollars and list-rate dollars, so an admin can quantify how much the account's negotiated discount actually saves on each SKU.

system.billing.usagesystem.billing.account_pricessystem.billing.list_prices
queries/cost/cost_actual_vs_list_by_sku.sql View source ↗ 
/* databricks_audit:cost_actual_vs_list_by_sku */
SELECT u.cloud, u.sku_name, u.usage_unit, u.billing_origin_product, u.currency_code,
       SUM(u.usage_quantity)                       AS net_usage_quantity,
       SUM(u.usage_quantity * ap.negotiated_rate)  AS net_negotiated_cost,
       SUM(u.usage_quantity * lp.list_rate)        AS net_list_cost
FROM system.billing.usage u
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT sku_name, cloud, currency_code, usage_unit, price_start_time, price_end_time,
         CAST(pricing.default AS DOUBLE) AS negotiated_rate   -- account_prices: pricing.default ONLY (no effective_list)
  FROM system.billing.account_prices
) ap
  ON  u.sku_name      = ap.sku_name
  AND u.cloud         = ap.cloud
  AND u.currency_code = ap.currency_code
  AND u.usage_unit    = ap.usage_unit
  AND u.usage_date    >= DATE(ap.price_start_time)
  AND (ap.price_end_time IS NULL OR u.usage_date < DATE(ap.price_end_time))   -- active rows carry NULL end_time
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT sku_name, cloud, currency_code, usage_unit, price_start_time, price_end_time,
         CAST(pricing.effective_list.default AS DOUBLE) AS list_rate   -- list_prices: effective_list.default
  FROM system.billing.list_prices
) lp
  ON  u.sku_name      = lp.sku_name
  AND u.cloud         = lp.cloud
  AND u.currency_code = lp.currency_code
  AND u.usage_unit    = lp.usage_unit
  AND u.usage_date    >= DATE(lp.price_start_time)
  AND (lp.price_end_time IS NULL OR u.usage_date < DATE(lp.price_end_time))   -- same active-row predicate
WHERE u.usage_date >= dateadd(day, -:period_days, current_date())
  AND u.usage_date < current_date()
  AND upper(u.usage_unit) = 'DBU'   -- price only DBU rows against a per-DBU rate; never blend bytes/hours/tokens
GROUP BY u.cloud, u.sku_name, u.usage_unit, u.billing_origin_product, u.currency_code
Why it matters

This is the proof that the account's negotiated Databricks contract is actually being realized: for every DBU-billed SKU it lines up what the account pays at its negotiated rate against public list, so the per-SKU gap (net_list_cost − net_negotiated_cost) is the dollar saving the discount delivers and feeds the discount-realization % and savings headline on the Pricing and Allocation tab. A SKU where the two are equal — or where net_negotiated_cost is null while DBUs are large — flags a SKU the negotiated agreement is not covering, which is real leakage against the contract. Because both dollar bases rely on unverified STRING-to-DOUBLE casts, every figure is labelled negotiated/list-unverified and must not be promoted to a billed headline until the casts are confirmed on-workspace.

What it does
  • For each SKU, totals your DBUs and prices them two ways — at your account's negotiated rate and at public list.
  • The gap between the two is the dollar saving your contract actually delivers on that SKU.
  • A SKU with usage but no negotiated price shows up as a coverage gap (blank), not as zero — so leakage against the contract stays visible.
How to read the output
ColumnHow to read it
cloudCloud provider the SKU ran on (aws/azure/gcp); part of the grain and a price-join key, so the same sku_name can appear once per cloud.
sku_nameThe billed SKU. The unit of comparison for negotiated-vs-list; a SKU with net_usage_quantity but null costs has no matching active price row (coverage gap).
usage_unitAlways 'DBU' here (the WHERE clause filters upper(usage_unit)='DBU') — confirms the rate applied is a per-DBU rate, not bytes/hours/tokens.
billing_origin_productProduct line that generated the usage (JOBS/SQL/DLT/MODEL_SERVING/…); part of the GROUP BY so a SKU split across products yields multiple rows. Blank = unattributed, not zero.
currency_codeCurrency of the price rows joined; also a join key. Never sum costs across differing currency_code values.
net_usage_quantitySUM(usage_quantity) in DBUs across all record_types over the window — the net metered consumption. DBUs, NOT dollars.
net_negotiated_costSUM(usage_quantity × account_prices negotiated_rate) — dollars the account pays at its contracted rate. NEGOTIATED-UNVERIFIED; NULL if no negotiated price row matched.
net_list_costSUM(usage_quantity × list_prices effective_list rate) — dollars at public pre-discount list. LIST-UNVERIFIED; NULL if no list price row matched. net_list_cost − net_negotiated_cost is the realized saving.
Sample output
cloudsku_nameusage_unitnet_usage_quantitynet_negotiated_costnet_list_costcurrency_code
awsENTERPRISE_ALL_PURPOSE_COMPUTEDBU125005500.006875.00USD
awsENTERPRISE_SQL_COMPUTEDBU3000528.00660.00USD
azurePREMIUM_JOBS_SERVERLESS_COMPUTEDBU800NULLNULLUSD
Illustrative synthetic rows — not real account data
System tables it reads
TableRole in this query
system.billing.usageDriving fact table: net metered consumption. SUM(usage_quantity) over the trailing :period_days window (excluding today) across all record_types, filtered to upper(usage_unit)='DBU', grouped by cloud/sku_name/usage_unit/billing_origin_product/currency_code.
system.billing.account_pricesNegotiated (actual) rate source. Sub-selected to negotiated_rate = CAST(pricing.default AS DOUBLE) — pricing.default ONLY, no effective_list — and LEFT-joined point-in-time to each usage row on cloud+currency_code+usage_unit+sku_name within the active price window.
system.billing.list_pricesPublic list (pre-discount) rate source. Sub-selected to list_rate = CAST(pricing.effective_list.default AS DOUBLE) and LEFT-joined on the same four keys and the same active-price-window predicate to produce net_list_cost.
Caveats & gotchas
  • Both dollar columns are NEGOTIATED/LIST-UNVERIFIED: account_prices.pricing.default and list_prices.pricing.effective_list.default are documented as object/STRING and the CAST(... AS DOUBLE) is not confirmed on this workspace — carry every figure as unverified, never promote to a billed usd_monthly headline.
  • usage_quantity / net_usage_quantity is DBUs, NOT dollars — only net_negotiated_cost and net_list_cost are dollars (and those are unverified).
  • TWO price bases: negotiated comes ONLY from account_prices.pricing.default; list comes ONLY from list_prices.pricing.effective_list.default. account_prices has NO effective_list — do not read it there.
  • Price-window predicate MUST be (price_end_time IS NULL OR usage_date < DATE(price_end_time)); active rows carry NULL end_time. Dropping the NULL branch silently zeroes recent usage.
  • Join keys are cloud + currency_code + usage_unit + sku_name, NOT sku_name alone — multi-cloud/multi-currency accounts have several price rows per sku_name and mis-join on sku_name alone.
  • Both price tables are LEFT-joined: a SKU with no matching active price row keeps net_usage_quantity but returns NULL cost — that is a priced-coverage gap, read it as 'not priced', never as $0.
  • Filtered to upper(usage_unit)='DBU' so bytes/hours/tokens are never priced against a per-DBU rate; non-DBU spend is excluded entirely from this result.
  • net_usage_quantity SUMs usage_quantity across ALL record_types (ORIGINAL/RETRACTION/RESTATEMENT) so corrections already net out — never re-net or filter to ORIGINAL.
  • Trailing :period_days window: usage_date >= dateadd(day, -:period_days, current_date()) AND < current_date() — excludes today (incomplete billing); treat the most recent day as provisional (ingestion lag).
  • currency_code is assumed present on usage rows; if absent, multi-currency disambiguation is lost — disclosed, not silently merged.
  • Requires Unity Catalog with system.billing enabled and SELECT granted; a disabled/unpermissioned table raises TABLE_OR_VIEW_NOT_FOUND (not assessed), and a feature the account doesn't use is a valid empty result — empty != error != $0.
  • No masking and no workspace-name resolution here — this rollup is SKU/cloud/product/currency grained, not identity- or workspace-grained.
→
What you do next
Rank SKUs by the net_list_cost − net_negotiated_cost gap to see where the negotiated contract actually saves money; feed the per-SKU discount-realization % into the Pricing and Allocation tab, and chase any SKU with net_usage_quantity but null costs as a priced-coverage gap (missing active price row), never as $0. Confirm the STRING-to-DOUBLE casts on this workspace before promoting any dollar figure.
All Cost & Billing ↑ Next →Per-Job DBU Cost Rankingcost_by_job
Overview / Cost & Billing / cost_by_job
02
Cost & Billing cost_by_job ★ first-audit pick

Per-Job DBU Cost Ranking

Net DBU consumption and distinct run count per job per day, split by workspace, cloud, product line, and classic-vs-serverless placement.

system.billing.usage
queries/cost/cost_by_job.sql View source ↗ 
/* databricks_audit:cost_by_job */
SELECT usage_date, cloud, workspace_id, billing_origin_product,
       usage_metadata.job_id          AS job_id,
       product_features.is_serverless AS is_serverless,
       SUM(usage_quantity) AS net_usage_quantity,
       COUNT(DISTINCT usage_metadata.job_run_id) AS distinct_runs
FROM system.billing.usage
WHERE usage_date >= dateadd(day, -:period_days, current_date())
  AND usage_date < current_date()
  AND usage_unit = 'DBU'
  AND usage_metadata.job_id IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY usage_date, cloud, workspace_id, billing_origin_product,
         usage_metadata.job_id, product_features.is_serverless
Why it matters

Jobs compute is usually the largest single product line on a Databricks bill, and this query pinpoints exactly which job_id drives the DBUs so an admin can target the worst offenders instead of the whole account. The is_serverless split lets you dollarize the jobs-on-all-purpose / classic-vs-serverless placement premium -- the same workload can cost materially more depending on where it runs. Pairing net_usage_quantity with distinct_runs surfaces failed-run and over-scheduled waste (many runs burning DBUs with little to show), which is often pure recoverable spend once the DBUs are priced.

What it does
  • Adds up the DBUs each job burned, day by day, over the look-back window.
  • Counts only jobs-compute usage — interactive notebook and SQL-editor lines are left out — so this is scheduled-job spend, nothing else.
  • Splits every job's usage by workspace and by classic-vs-serverless placement, and counts how many runs produced it.
  • Hands you the raw per-job totals to sort — the biggest spenders, and jobs racking up DBUs over many runs, rise to the top.
How to read the output
ColumnHow to read it
usage_dateThe calendar day (DATE) the job consumption was metered. One row per job per day per placement, so the same job_id appears on multiple dates -- sum across dates for a period total.
cloudThe cloud provider for the usage (AWS/AZURE/GCP). Part of the grain and a required key when later joining price tables.
workspace_idThe workspace that ran the job. NULL for account-level SKUs; resolve to dev/uat/prod via cost_workspace_names downstream, otherwise it reads as an opaque numeric id.
billing_origin_productThe product line billed (typically JOBS for jobs-compute, but can be DLT or others). No published closed enum; a blank value is unattributed, not zero.
job_idThe Databricks job identifier (from usage_metadata.job_id). This is a stable id but carries no name/owner -- join to system.lakeflow.jobs (latest by change_time) for name and run_as.
is_serverlessproduct_features.is_serverless: TRUE for jobs-serverless, FALSE for classic jobs-compute. This is the placement-premium signal that separates serverless from classic runs of the same job.
net_usage_quantityNet DBUs consumed by that job on that day/placement, SUM(usage_quantity) across all record_types (already correction-netted). DBUs, NOT dollars -- multiply by a per-DBU rate to dollarize.
distinct_runsCOUNT(DISTINCT usage_metadata.job_run_id) -- how many separate job runs generated the DBUs that day. High DBUs over few runs = heavy runs; high runs = frequent scheduling; pair with failed-run analysis to size retry/failure waste.
Sample output
usage_dateworkspace_idjob_idis_serverlessnet_usage_quantitydistinct_runs
2026-07-041234567890123456849302false412.7524
2026-07-041234567890123456849302true58.103
2026-07-049876543210987654771044false1180.001
Illustrative synthetic rows — not real account data
System tables it reads
TableRole in this query
system.billing.usageThe sole table read. Provides the billed-consumption fact rows; the query filters to usage_unit='DBU' and usage_metadata.job_id IS NOT NULL, then SUMs usage_quantity (net DBUs) and counts DISTINCT usage_metadata.job_run_id, grouped by usage_date, cloud, workspace_id, billing_origin_product, usage_metadata.job_id, and product_features.is_serverless.
Caveats & gotchas
  • usage_metadata.job_id populates for jobs-compute only (classic AND serverless); it is NULL for interactive / SQL-editor lines, which are excluded by the WHERE job_id IS NOT NULL filter -- this is job spend only, not the whole bill.
  • net_usage_quantity is DBUs, NOT dollars. Multiply by a per-DBU list/negotiated rate (join system.billing.list_prices on cloud+currency_code+usage_unit+sku_name, with the (price_end_time IS NULL OR usage < price_end_time) window predicate) to get money -- and those price casts are unverified.
  • SUM(usage_quantity) is a NET figure across all record_type values (ORIGINAL/RETRACTION/RESTATEMENT); the query does not filter to ORIGINAL, so corrections already net out -- do not re-net downstream.
  • Date window is a trailing :period_days ending yesterday: usage_date >= dateadd(day,-:period_days,current_date()) AND usage_date < current_date(). Today is excluded and the most recent day is provisional due to billing populate lag (ingestion_date, not usage_date, is the freshness signal).
  • No names here. Billing carries no job name or owner -- join job_id -> system.lakeflow.jobs (SCD2, take latest by change_time) downstream for name + run_as. workspace_id stays a numeric id unless resolved via cost_workspace_names.
  • workspace_id is NULL for account-level SKUs (kept as 'not workspace-attributable'), and billing_origin_product has no published closed enum -- a blank value is 'unattributed', not zero.
  • Empty != zero != error. An account with no jobs simply yields no rows; a disabled/non-permissioned system table raises TABLE_OR_VIEW_NOT_FOUND -- both mean 'not assessed', never $0.
  • Requires Unity Catalog with system.billing enabled and SELECT granted. No masking is applied in this query (no PII columns are selected). Only usage_unit = 'DBU' rows are counted.
→
What you do next
Rank jobs by net_usage_quantity within a workspace/day, then resolve the top job_ids to names and run_as via system.lakeflow.jobs (SCD2, latest by change_time), dollarize the DBUs through list_prices, and target the biggest classic-jobs-compute lines and high-run-count offenders for right-sizing, scheduling, or moving off all-purpose compute.
← PreviousNegotiated vs List Discount Realizationcost_actual_vs_list_by_sku All Cost & Billing ↑ Next →Chargeback Coverage by Tagcost_chargeback_by_tag
Overview / Cost & Billing / cost_chargeback_by_tag
03
Cost & Billing cost_chargeback_by_tag ★ first-audit pick

Chargeback Coverage by Tag

Net DBU consumption and record count broken down by every custom_tags key/value pair per usage_date, cloud, workspace, and product line — with untagged usage retained as NULL-key rows so the "% untagged" denominator is real.

system.billing.usage
queries/cost/cost_chargeback_by_tag.sql View source ↗ 
/* databricks_audit:cost_chargeback_by_tag */
SELECT usage_date, cloud, workspace_id, billing_origin_product, tag_key, tag_value,
       SUM(usage_quantity) AS net_usage_quantity,
       COUNT(*) AS record_count
FROM system.billing.usage
     LATERAL VIEW OUTER explode(custom_tags) t AS tag_key, tag_value
WHERE usage_date >= dateadd(day, -:period_days, current_date())
  AND usage_date < current_date()
GROUP BY usage_date, cloud, workspace_id, billing_origin_product, tag_key, tag_value
Why it matters

Chargeback and showback only work if spend is attributable to a team, project, or cost center via tags — and this query is how an admin proves whether that attribution actually exists. The NULL-key rows produced by the OUTER explode quantify the DBUs that carry no tag at all (the % untagged spend that no one can be billed for), while counting distinct tag_values per tag_key exposes the trap where a single umbrella tag covers ~100% of DBUs yet resolves to one cost center, giving zero real team-level chargeback. On an account spending six or seven figures, untagged or falsely-"100%-tagged" DBUs are money that lands in a shared bucket and gets cross-subsidized instead of billed back to the workload that caused it.

What it does
  • Breaks your DBU usage down by every cost-allocation tag (key and value) attached to it.
  • Deliberately keeps untagged usage as its own bucket, so you get an honest '% of spend nobody can be billed for' number.
  • Cuts the tag rollup per workspace and product line — enough to spot tag drift, and to catch an umbrella tag that 'covers everything' yet names a single cost centre.
How to read the output
ColumnHow to read it
usage_dateThe calendar day (DATE) the consumption was metered. Today is always excluded; treat the most recent day as provisional due to billing populate lag.
cloudCloud provider for the row (e.g. AWS/AZURE/GCP). Part of the grain and a required join key elsewhere — never collapse tags across clouds without keeping this.
workspace_idNumeric workspace the usage belongs to; lets chargeback be cut per workspace. NULL for account-level SKUs (not workspace-attributable). Resolve to dev/uat/prod names downstream via cost_workspace_names.
billing_origin_productProduct line that generated the usage (JOBS/SQL/DLT/MODEL_SERVING/…). No published closed enum, so treat literals as unverified; a blank value is 'unattributed', not zero.
tag_keyOne key from the custom_tags map (customer-defined — never hardcode key names). NULL means the source usage row carried no tags at all: this is the untagged bucket that drives the % untagged metric.
tag_valueThe value for that tag_key on the row. NULL alongside a NULL tag_key marks untagged usage. Count DISTINCT tag_value per tag_key downstream to detect an umbrella key that covers everything but resolves to a single cost center.
net_usage_quantitySUM(usage_quantity) for the group — net DBUs/units (in usage_unit, NOT dollars) across all record_types. Multiply by a price rate downstream to dollarize. Note different usage_units are summed together here, so scope by product/SKU if you need a single unit.
record_countCOUNT(*) of exploded usage rows in the group — a volume/skew sanity check (how many metered slices carry this tag), not a monetary figure.
Sample output
usage_datecloudworkspace_idbilling_origin_producttag_keytag_valuenet_usage_quantityrecord_count
2026-07-04AWS1234567890123456JOBScost_centerdata-platform842.5037
2026-07-04AWS1234567890123456SQLteamanalytics318.7522
2026-07-04AWS1234567890123456DLTNULLNULL156.209
Illustrative synthetic rows — not real account data
System tables it reads
TableRole in this query
system.billing.usageThe sole source table. Provides the billed-consumption rows whose custom_tags MAP is exploded (OUTER) into one row per tag key/value; usage_quantity (DBUs) is summed per tag and per (usage_date, cloud, workspace_id, billing_origin_product) cut. OUTER explode keeps rows with empty/NULL custom_tags so untagged spend still appears.
Caveats & gotchas
  • DBUs, not dollars: net_usage_quantity is DBUs/bytes/hours/tokens in usage_unit — no price join here. Multiply by a rate (list_prices/account_prices) downstream to get money, and never sum across different usage_unit families.
  • OUTER explode is deliberate: untagged rows appear with tag_key/tag_value = NULL so they anchor the '% untagged' denominator — pair with cost_totals_by_sku_day for the account total; don't drop the NULL rows.
  • Do NOT report a bare '100% tagged'. On this account one umbrella key can cover ~100% of DBUs yet give NO team-level chargeback (single cost center) — count distinct tag_value per tag_key to expose that.
  • Tags are dynamic: custom_tags keys are customer-defined and vary per customer — never hardcode key names. Tags originate from compute-resource tags AND serverless usage policies.
  • Net across corrections: net_usage_quantity SUMs usage_quantity over all record_types (ORIGINAL/RETRACTION/RESTATEMENT already net out) — never filter to ORIGINAL and never re-net downstream.
  • Date window: trailing :period_days days, excludes today (incomplete billing); the most recent day is provisional due to populate lag (ingestion_date, not usage_date, is the freshness signal).
  • workspace_id is NULL for account-level SKUs (not workspace-attributable) and is a raw numeric id — names resolve only via cost_workspace_names downstream. Cut chargeback per workspace but keep cloud in the key.
  • billing_origin_product has no published closed enum (unverified literals); a blank value is 'unattributed', not zero.
  • Empty != zero != error: a valid empty result is not $0, and a Preview/disabled or unpermissioned system table raises TABLE_OR_VIEW_NOT_FOUND — both are expected, treat as 'not assessed', not a fabricated 0. Requires Unity Catalog + system.billing enabled + SELECT granted.
  • No masking is applied in this query — tag values are surfaced as-is (they are not PII-bearing here); the domain's email/name masking applies to the identity query, not this one.
→
What you do next
Rank tag_keys by SUM(net_usage_quantity) and count distinct tag_value per tag_key: a key that covers ~100% of DBUs but has only one distinct value is an umbrella/single-cost-center tag giving no team-level chargeback — flag it and require a team/cost-center dimension. Compute % untagged DBUs by comparing the NULL-tag_key rows against the account total from cost_totals_by_sku_day, then chase down the owners of the largest untagged slices to enforce a tagging policy.
← PreviousPer-Job DBU Cost Rankingcost_by_job All Cost & Billing ↑ Next →Dollarizing DBUs at List Pricecost_dollarized_by_sku_day
Overview / Cost & Billing / cost_dollarized_by_sku_day
04
Cost & Billing cost_dollarized_by_sku_day ★ first-audit pick

Dollarizing DBUs at List Price

Net DBU (and other-unit) consumption multiplied by the point-in-time list rate to produce a pre-discount list-dollar figure (net_list_cost) per usage_date x cloud x SKU x product x usage_type x unit x currency.

system.billing.usagesystem.billing.list_prices
queries/cost/cost_dollarized_by_sku_day.sql View source ↗ 
/* databricks_audit:cost_dollarized_by_sku_day */
-- NEEDS CONFIRMATION: list_rate path pricing.effective_list.default is UNVERIFIED.
-- Until confirmed, dollarize in-engine off pricing_list_prices_raw (raw JSON) instead.
SELECT u.usage_date, u.cloud, u.sku_name, u.billing_origin_product, u.usage_type, u.usage_unit,
       lp.currency_code,
       SUM(u.usage_quantity)               AS net_usage_quantity,
       SUM(u.usage_quantity * lp.list_rate) AS net_list_cost
FROM system.billing.usage u
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT sku_name, cloud, currency_code, usage_unit, price_start_time, price_end_time,
         CAST(pricing.effective_list.default AS DOUBLE) AS list_rate   -- <-- UNVERIFIED path
  FROM system.billing.list_prices
) lp
  ON u.sku_name = lp.sku_name
 AND u.cloud    = lp.cloud
 AND u.usage_end_time >= lp.price_start_time
 AND (lp.price_end_time IS NULL OR u.usage_end_time < lp.price_end_time)
WHERE u.usage_date >= dateadd(day, -:period_days, current_date())
  AND u.usage_date < current_date()
GROUP BY u.usage_date, u.cloud, u.sku_name, u.billing_origin_product, u.usage_type, u.usage_unit, lp.currency_code
Why it matters

This is the query that turns raw DBU consumption into money, so it drives every headline cost chart, dollar-denominated anomaly series, and chargeback rollup an admin builds. The stakes are twofold: get the number and get the caveat right. net_list_cost is list (pre-discount) price only, DBU-only, and excludes cloud infra/egress, so it overstates what the account actually pays; worse, the list_rate path (pricing.effective_list.default cast to DOUBLE) is unverified on-workspace, so a silently-wrong cast could mis-scale every dollar figure downstream. Used carefully it prioritizes which SKUs and product lines to attack; used blindly it produces a confidently wrong bill.

What it does
  • Adds up all your metered usage — DBUs, plus any storage bytes, hours or tokens — for every day in the look-back window.
  • Prices each day's usage at the list rate that was in force that day, turning raw DBUs into list dollars.
  • Breaks the money down by day, cloud, SKU, product line and unit, so you can see which SKUs and product lines drive the bill and how it moves day to day.
  • Where a usage row has no matching price, it leaves the dollar figure blank rather than guessing — an unpriced gap shows up instead of a wrong number.
How to read the output
ColumnHow to read it
usage_dateThe billed usage day (DATE). One row per day per SKU/product/usage_type/unit/currency combination within the trailing window; today is excluded.
cloudCloud provider for the usage (e.g. AWS/AZURE/GCP). Also a join key to the price window, so pricing is cloud-correct.
sku_nameThe Databricks billing SKU. The primary price-join key; each SKU carries its own list rate.
billing_origin_productProduct line that generated the usage (JOBS/SQL/DLT/MODEL_SERVING/DEFAULT_STORAGE/...). No closed enum; blank means unattributed, not zero.
usage_typeNature of the metered consumption (COMPUTE_TIME/STORAGE_SPACE/TOKEN/GPU_TIME/...). Kept as a group dimension so unit families are not blended.
usage_unitUnit of net_usage_quantity (DBU, STORAGE_SPACE bytes, hours, TOKEN, ...). Grouped so different unit families are never summed together.
currency_codeCurrency of net_list_cost, sourced from the list_prices side of the join. NULL when no price window matched (unpriced row).
net_usage_quantitySUM(usage_quantity) across all record_types for the group: net metered consumption in usage_unit (DBUs/bytes/hours/tokens). This is NOT dollars.
net_list_costSUM(usage_quantity * list_rate): net consumption valued at the point-in-time LIST rate. Dollars, but pre-discount, DBU-only, and UNVERIFIED (list_rate path unconfirmed). NULL where no price matched.
Sample output
usage_datecloudsku_namebilling_origin_productusage_unitnet_usage_quantitynet_list_cost
2026-07-01AWSENTERPRISE_ALL_PURPOSE_COMPUTEJOBSDBU1234.5000678.9750
2026-07-01AWSENTERPRISE_SQL_COMPUTESQLDBU4200.00002940.0000
2026-07-01AZUREENTERPRISE_JOBS_SERVERLESS_COMPUTEJOBSDBU800.0000NULL
Illustrative synthetic rows — not real account data
System tables it reads
TableRole in this query
system.billing.usageThe billed-consumption fact table (aliased u); provides usage_date, cloud, sku_name, billing_origin_product, usage_type, usage_unit, usage_end_time, and usage_quantity that is SUMmed across all record_types into net_usage_quantity and dollarized. Also carries the date-window filter.
system.billing.list_pricesThe public list (pre-discount) price history (aliased lp in a subquery); supplies currency_code and list_rate (CAST of pricing.effective_list.default to DOUBLE), joined by sku_name + cloud within the [price_start_time, price_end_time) window to price each usage row. Path is unverified.
Caveats & gotchas
  • LIST price only: net_list_cost is pre-discount, DBU-only, and excludes cloud infra/egress; the engine applies the negotiated discount_factor downstream. It is never a billed headline number.
  • UNVERIFIED price path: list_rate casts pricing.effective_list.default to DOUBLE, but docs type effective_list only as an object (and pricing.default as STRING). Until confirmed on-workspace, dollarize in-engine off pricing_list_prices_raw (raw JSON) instead of trusting net_list_cost.
  • DBUs are not dollars: net_usage_quantity is DBUs/bytes/hours/tokens in usage_unit, NOT money. Only net_list_cost is a dollar figure, and only a LIST/unverified one.
  • Point-in-time price predicate: the join uses (price_end_time IS NULL OR usage_end_time < price_end_time). price_end_time NULL means currently-active; dropping the NULL branch silently zeroes recent usage.
  • Net across corrections: SUM(usage_quantity) spans all record_type values (ORIGINAL/RETRACTION/RESTATEMENT) which already net out; the query does NOT filter to ORIGINAL and you must not re-net downstream.
  • LEFT JOIN on price: rows with no matching price window get list_rate NULL, so net_list_cost is NULL for those (usage still counted in net_usage_quantity). NULL net_list_cost means 'unpriced', not $0.
  • Join keys are sku_name + cloud + time window only; currency_code comes from the price side. usage_unit is selected in the subquery but NOT used in the ON clause, so multi-cloud/multi-currency accounts rely on cloud alone to avoid fan-out or mis-pricing (README recommends keying on cloud + currency_code + usage_unit + sku_name).
  • Date window: trailing :period_days ending yesterday (usage_date >= current_date - :period_days AND < current_date). Today is excluded because billing is incomplete; treat the most recent day as provisional due to populate lag (ingestion_date is the freshness signal).
  • Availability: both tables are GA but Unity-Catalog-only, require system.billing enabled and SELECT granted. A feature the account doesn't use yields a valid empty result, not $0; a disabled/non-permissioned table raises TABLE_OR_VIEW_NOT_FOUND. Empty != zero != error.
  • billing_origin_product has no published closed enum; a blank value is 'unattributed', not zero. Do not hardcode literals as a verified set.
→
What you do next
Use net_list_cost as the pre-discount, list-price dollar view for headline cost and anomaly charts, but do not report it as billed spend: cross-check the list_rate against pricing_list_prices_raw (dollarize in-engine off the raw JSON) before trusting any figure, and remember it excludes cloud infra/egress and the account's negotiated discount.
← PreviousChargeback Coverage by Tagcost_chargeback_by_tag All Cost & Billing ↑ Next →Serverless, Photon & Tier Premiumscost_premium_serverless_photon
Overview / Cost & Billing / cost_premium_serverless_photon
05
Cost & Billing cost_premium_serverless_photon ★ first-audit pick

Serverless, Photon & Tier Premiums

Net DBU consumption per usage_date x cloud x SKU x product line, split out by the serverless, Photon, jobs/SQL/DLT tier, and serverless performance-target choices so each premium lever can be sized.

system.billing.usage
queries/cost/cost_premium_serverless_photon.sql View source ↗ 
/* databricks_audit:cost_premium_serverless_photon */
SELECT usage_date, cloud, sku_name, billing_origin_product,
       product_features.is_serverless      AS is_serverless,
       product_features.is_photon          AS is_photon,
       product_features.jobs_tier          AS jobs_tier,
       product_features.sql_tier           AS sql_tier,
       product_features.dlt_tier           AS dlt_tier,
       product_features.performance_target AS performance_target,
       SUM(usage_quantity) AS net_usage_quantity
FROM system.billing.usage
WHERE usage_date >= dateadd(day, -:period_days, current_date())
  AND usage_date < current_date()
GROUP BY usage_date, cloud, sku_name, billing_origin_product,
         product_features.is_serverless, product_features.is_photon, product_features.jobs_tier,
         product_features.sql_tier, product_features.dlt_tier, product_features.performance_target
Why it matters

Serverless, Photon, DLT tiers, and performance-optimized serverless all carry a per-DBU premium over their classic/baseline equivalents in exchange for speed or convenience. This query isolates exactly how many DBUs are flowing through each premium option so an admin can size the delta and, after dollarizing via list_prices, decide whether the premium is buying real value or is just an unexamined default. A single always-Photon SQL warehouse or a performance-optimized serverless job left on the fast tier can quietly inflate the bill by a large multiple versus the standard tier.

What it does
  • Totals your DBUs and splits them by the premium levers: serverless-vs-classic, Photon on/off, and the jobs / SQL / DLT tier and serverless performance target.
  • Shows how much usage flows through each premium option, so you can price the delta against the cheaper baseline.
  • Each lever is its own column, so a single always-Photon warehouse or a perf-optimized serverless job stands out at a glance.
How to read the output
ColumnHow to read it
usage_dateThe billing day (DATE) the consumption was metered; the most recent day in-window is provisional due to populate lag.
cloudCloud provider for the usage (e.g. AWS/AZURE/GCP); part of the grain and a required key when later joining price tables.
sku_nameThe billed SKU string; premium vs baseline often shows in the SKU name itself (e.g. a '(PHOTON)' suffix), so use it to pair a premium row with its cheaper counterpart.
billing_origin_productProduct line that generated the usage (JOBS/SQL/DLT/MODEL_SERVING/...); no closed enum, and blank means unattributed rather than zero.
is_serverlessWhether the usage ran on serverless compute (true) vs classic (false); NULL where the serverless/classic distinction is unavailable. The core serverless-vs-classic premium split.
is_photonWhether Photon acceleration was active (true) vs not (false); NULL where unknown. Cross-checkable against sku_name LIKE '%(PHOTON)%' as a heuristic.
jobs_tierThe Jobs compute tier for JOBS usage; NULL where not applicable.
sql_tierThe DBSQL warehouse tier (e.g. classic/pro/serverless) for SQL usage; NULL where not applicable.
dlt_tierThe DLT/Lakeflow pipeline tier (e.g. core/pro/advanced) for DLT usage; NULL where not applicable — the DLT-tier optimization lever.
performance_targetThe serverless performance target (e.g. performance-optimized vs standard); NULL for all non-serverless workloads. Sizes the perf-optimized-serverless premium.
net_usage_quantityNet summed usage_quantity (in the row's usage_unit, typically DBUs) across all record_types for this combination — a DBU quantity, NOT dollars; multiply by a list rate to dollarize.
Sample output
usage_datecloudsku_namebilling_origin_productis_serverlessis_photonnet_usage_quantity
2026-07-04AWSENTERPRISE_SQL_COMPUTE_SERVERLESS_(PHOTON)SQLtruetrue1842.75
2026-07-04AWSENTERPRISE_SQL_PRO_COMPUTESQLfalsefalse612.40
2026-07-04AZUREPREMIUM_JOBS_SERVERLESS_COMPUTEJOBStruefalse308.10
Illustrative synthetic rows — not real account data
System tables it reads
TableRole in this query
system.billing.usageThe sole source. Provides usage_date, cloud, sku_name, billing_origin_product, the metered usage_quantity (summed to net_usage_quantity), and the product_features STRUCT whose subfields (is_serverless, is_photon, jobs_tier, sql_tier, dlt_tier, performance_target) are exploded into the group-by to isolate each cost lever.
Caveats & gotchas
  • DBUs, not dollars: net_usage_quantity is summed usage_quantity (DBUs/units in usage_unit), never money — multiply by a list_prices rate to get the premium in dollars, and that list-rate cast is itself unverified.
  • product_features is sparse: is_serverless / is_photon / jobs_tier / sql_tier / dlt_tier / performance_target are NULL wherever that choice doesn't apply (e.g. performance_target is NULL for all non-serverless workloads). A NULL is 'not applicable', not 'false'.
  • Net across corrections: SUM(usage_quantity) spans all record_type values (ORIGINAL/RETRACTION/RESTATEMENT) — the query does not filter to ORIGINAL, so figures are already net. Do not re-net downstream.
  • is_photon is a struct flag; it can be cross-checked against sku_name LIKE '%(PHOTON)%', but that is a string-pattern heuristic, not authoritative.
  • Date window excludes today: rows span usage_date >= current_date - :period_days up to but not including current_date; treat the most recent day as provisional due to billing populate lag (ingestion_date, not usage_date, is the freshness signal).
  • Empty != zero != error: a feature the account doesn't use yields no rows (valid empty), while a disabled/non-permissioned system table raises TABLE_OR_VIEW_NOT_FOUND — neither means $0.
  • Availability: requires Unity Catalog with system.billing enabled and SELECT granted; usage_quantity mixes usage_unit families (DBU vs STORAGE_SPACE vs TOKEN) — do not sum across units when interpreting.
  • billing_origin_product has no published closed enum; a blank value is 'unattributed', not zero. No masking or workspace-name join is present in this query (no workspace_id column is selected).
→
What you do next
Pick the biggest net_usage_quantity rows and compare premium against baseline variants of the same billing_origin_product: for each SKU family, contrast is_serverless=true vs false, is_photon=true vs false, and the dlt_tier / performance_target choice. Multiply the DBU delta by the SKU's list rate (join system.billing.list_prices on cloud+currency_code+usage_unit+sku_name with the point-in-time price predicate) to dollarize the serverless/Photon/tier premium, then decide whether the perf gain justifies it or whether workloads should be moved to the cheaper tier.
← PreviousDollarizing DBUs at List Pricecost_dollarized_by_sku_day All Cost & Billing ↑ Next →Your Real Negotiated Price Basiscost_account_prices_raw
Overview / Cost & Billing / cost_account_prices_raw
06
Cost & Billing cost_account_prices_raw

Your Real Negotiated Price Basis

The full history of your account's actual negotiated per-SKU rates, one row per price change, kept raw so downstream logic can price each usage record in its own validity window.

system.billing.account_prices
queries/cost/cost_account_prices_raw.sql View source ↗ 
/* databricks_audit:cost_account_prices_raw */
SELECT price_start_time, price_end_time, account_id, sku_name, cloud, currency_code, usage_unit,
       CAST(pricing.default AS STRING) AS pricing_default
FROM system.billing.account_prices
Why it matters

This is the only table that carries the discounted rate your account really pays, as opposed to public list price — so it is the ground truth for proving how much your negotiated contract actually saves. Feeding it into actual-vs-list comparisons quantifies discount realization per SKU and flags SKUs where you are paying closer to list than your contract implies. Because it is pulled raw across the full price history (not a single current rate), every usage record can be priced in the exact window that was in force when it was billed, which prevents mis-dating spend around a rate change. Treat every dollar derived from it as negotiated-rate-unverified until the STRING-to-numeric cast is confirmed on your workspace.

What it does
  • Returns the complete negotiated-price history for your account — one row for every time the rate on a SKU changed, not a single current snapshot.
  • Exposes the account's real negotiated rate (pricing_default), which is the discounted price you pay, distinct from public list price.
  • Stamps each rate with the time window it was valid for, so the correct price can be matched to any past usage record.
  • Carries the negotiated multi-cloud / multi-currency identity of each SKU (cloud, currency_code, usage_unit) so rates are never blended across clouds or currencies.
How to read the output
ColumnHow to read it
price_start_timeWhen this negotiated rate became effective. Use it as the lower bound of the price window when matching a usage record to its rate.
price_end_timeWhen this rate stopped being effective. `NULL` means this is the currently-active negotiated price — you MUST treat NULL as active, never as expired or zero.
account_idThe Databricks account the negotiated rate belongs to. Constant for a single-account pull; confirms scope.
sku_nameThe billing SKU the rate applies to. Join on this together with cloud + currency_code + usage_unit, never on sku_name alone.
cloudThe cloud provider (AWS/AZURE/GCP) this rate is for. Part of the composite price key; multi-cloud accounts have several rows per SKU.
currency_codeThe currency the negotiated rate is denominated in. Part of the composite key; never compare or sum rates across currencies.
usage_unitThe unit the rate is priced per (typically DBU). Part of the composite key; ensures a DBU rate is only applied to DBU usage.
pricing_defaultThe negotiated per-unit rate itself, returned as a STRING (CAST AS STRING in the SQL). This is your actual discounted rate — but its numeric cast is unverified, so confirm it parses to a number before doing arithmetic. Note there is NO effective_list on this table; that lives on list_prices.
Sample output
price_start_timeprice_end_timeaccount_idsku_namecloudcurrency_codeusage_unitpricing_default
2025-01-01T00:00:00Z2025-06-30T00:00:00Za1b2c3d4-5e6f-7890-abcd-ef1234567890ENTERPRISE_ALL_PURPOSE_COMPUTEAWSUSDDBU0.44
2025-06-30T00:00:00ZNULLa1b2c3d4-5e6f-7890-abcd-ef1234567890ENTERPRISE_ALL_PURPOSE_COMPUTEAWSUSDDBU0.42
2025-03-15T00:00:00ZNULLa1b2c3d4-5e6f-7890-abcd-ef1234567890ENTERPRISE_SQL_COMPUTEAZUREEURDBU0.19
Illustrative synthetic rows — not real account data
System tables it reads
TableRole in this query
system.billing.account_pricesThe account's actual negotiated price history — one row per SKU negotiated-rate change with its [price_start_time, price_end_time) validity window. This query reads it raw (no join, no filter) to expose the negotiated `pricing.default` rate per SKU, cloud, currency, and unit.
Caveats & gotchas
  • DBUs are not dollars: pricing_default is a per-DBU RATE, not spend. It only becomes money when multiplied by DBU usage_quantity.
  • Unverified price path: pricing.default is documented as STRING and is returned by this query as STRING (CAST AS STRING); the numeric CAST to DOUBLE is not confirmed on-workspace, so every derived dollar stays labelled negotiated-rate-unverified and is never promoted to a billed headline.
  • price_end_time IS NULL means the currently-active negotiated price — downstream MUST treat NULL as active, never as expired or zero.
  • This table has ONLY pricing.default — there is NO pricing.effective_list here (that lives on list_prices). Actual-vs-list compares this default against list_prices.pricing.effective_list.default.
  • Join key is cloud + currency_code + usage_unit + sku_name, NOT sku_name alone — multi-cloud/multi-currency accounts have several rows per SKU and will fan out or mis-price if keyed on sku_name only.
  • Not date-windowed: unlike the consumption queries, this pulls the FULL negotiated-price history and ignores :period_days, on purpose — the engine needs every past window to price historical usage.
  • Availability: GA but Unity-Catalog-required with SELECT granted; an unavailable/disabled table raises TABLE_OR_VIEW_NOT_FOUND and should degrade to 'not assessed', never a fabricated $0.
  • Collected raw (one row per price change) by design — do not pre-aggregate or dedupe to 'latest'; the engine picks the right window per usage row itself.
→
What you do next
Confirm on your own workspace that pricing_default casts cleanly to a number, then join this history to system.billing.usage (on cloud + currency_code + usage_unit + sku_name, matching each usage record to its active price window) to compute your real negotiated spend and feed actual-vs-list discount-realization per SKU.
← PreviousServerless, Photon & Tier Premiumscost_premium_serverless_photon All Cost & Billing ↑ Next →Where the Money Goes by Productcost_by_billing_origin_product
Overview / Cost & Billing / cost_by_billing_origin_product
07
Cost & Billing cost_by_billing_origin_product

Where the Money Goes by Product

Net metered consumption over the trailing window, broken out by Databricks product line, unit of measure, and cloud, with a record count per group.

system.billing.usage
queries/cost/cost_by_billing_origin_product.sql View source ↗ 
/* databricks_audit:cost_by_billing_origin_product */
SELECT billing_origin_product, usage_unit, cloud,
       SUM(usage_quantity) AS net_usage_quantity,
       COUNT(*)            AS record_count
FROM system.billing.usage
WHERE usage_date >= dateadd(day, -:period_days, current_date())
  AND usage_date < current_date()
GROUP BY billing_origin_product, usage_unit, cloud
Why it matters

This is the product-mix Pareto for the entire account bill: it tells an admin which product lines (JOBS, SQL, DLT, MODEL_SERVING, VECTOR_SEARCH, DEFAULT_STORAGE, ...) actually dominate consumption, so cost-optimization effort lands on the 20% of products driving 80% of spend rather than on noise. Because the net figure sums across all billing corrections, the number is trustworthy and won't double-count restatements. A blank product line surfaces unattributed consumption that would otherwise hide in the total, and keeping each unit family on its own row prevents the classic error of adding DBUs, storage bytes, and tokens into a single meaningless sum.

What it does
  • Totals your net consumption per product line (JOBS / SQL / DLT / MODEL_SERVING / ...) so you can see which products drive the bill.
  • Keeps each unit family separate (DBU vs storage bytes vs hours vs TOKEN) on its own row, and further splits by cloud — units are never blended.
  • Nets out billing corrections automatically, so each figure is the trustworthy after-restatement quantity, not a raw or double-counted one.
  • Reports these as consumption quantities, not dollars — a per-product record_count shows how many usage records rolled into each line.
How to read the output
ColumnHow to read it
billing_origin_productThe Databricks product line that generated the usage (e.g. JOBS, SQL, DLT, MODEL_SERVING, DEFAULT_STORAGE). No published closed enum, so treat literals as unverified; a blank/NULL value means 'unattributed product', not zero.
usage_unitThe unit that net_usage_quantity is measured in (DBU, STORAGE_SPACE bytes, hours, TOKEN, ...). Rows with different units are on different scales and must never be summed together.
cloudThe cloud provider (AWS / AZURE / GCP) the usage was billed on; a product line can appear on more than one cloud.
net_usage_quantityTotal metered amount in usage_unit for this product/unit/cloud, summed across all record types so corrections already net out. This is DBUs/bytes/hours/tokens, NOT dollars.
record_countNumber of underlying usage records that rolled into this group; a high count with low quantity signals many small slices, useful as a sanity/coverage check.
Sample output
billing_origin_productusage_unitcloudnet_usage_quantityrecord_count
JOBSDBUAWS48213.512904
SQLDBUAWS19677.05321
DEFAULT_STORAGESTORAGE_SPACEAWS1099511627776842
Illustrative synthetic rows — not real account data
System tables it reads
TableRole in this query
system.billing.usageThe billed-consumption fact table read here; supplies billing_origin_product (product line), usage_unit, cloud, and usage_quantity, aggregated into net usage per group. It is the only table this query touches.
Caveats & gotchas
  • DBUs are not dollars: net_usage_quantity is DBUs/bytes/hours/tokens in usage_unit; multiply by a price rate (see the dollarized queries) to get money.
  • Never sum across usage_unit families - DBU, STORAGE_SPACE bytes, hours, and TOKEN are different scales and must be reported separately.
  • Already net of corrections: the sum spans all record_types (ORIGINAL/RETRACTION/RESTATEMENT), so never re-net downstream and never filter to record_type='ORIGINAL' for a net rollup.
  • A blank/NULL billing_origin_product means 'unattributed product', not zero; billing_origin_product has no published closed enum, so product literals are unverified.
  • Empty != zero != error: a product the account doesn't use simply yields no rows (a valid empty result, not $0); a disabled/non-permissioned system table raises TABLE_OR_VIEW_NOT_FOUND.
  • Date window is a trailing :period_days-day look-back that excludes today (today's billing is incomplete); treat the most recent day as provisional due to populate lag.
  • Requires Unity Catalog with system.billing enabled and SELECT granted; this query is account-wide and does not resolve workspace names.
→
What you do next
Rank the product lines by net_usage_quantity within each usage_unit to identify the 1-2 products dominating consumption, then drill into those (e.g. cost_by_job for JOBS, cost_by_serving_endpoint for MODEL_SERVING) and dollarize via the list-price join before deciding where to focus optimization.
← PreviousYour Real Negotiated Price Basiscost_account_prices_raw All Cost & Billing ↑ Next →Cost Per Cluster and Warehousecost_by_compute_resource
Overview / Cost & Billing / cost_by_compute_resource
08
Cost & Billing cost_by_compute_resource

Cost Per Cluster and Warehouse

A daily, per-workspace breakdown of net DBU consumption attributed to each individual cluster, SQL warehouse, and instance pool, split by product line and serverless-vs-classic.

system.billing.usage
queries/cost/cost_by_compute_resource.sql View source ↗ 
/* databricks_audit:cost_by_compute_resource */
SELECT usage_date, cloud, workspace_id, billing_origin_product,
       usage_metadata.cluster_id        AS cluster_id,
       usage_metadata.warehouse_id      AS warehouse_id,
       usage_metadata.instance_pool_id  AS instance_pool_id,
       product_features.is_serverless   AS is_serverless,
